main_functions.description: Handles creation of a new user account with required fields, validation, and inline error messages. Covers field format validation, password rules, confirmation matching, and detection of duplicate emails.

sub_functions.sub_function_name: Field Format and Composition Validation (Email and Password)
sub_functions.description: Validates email format and password composition rules, including length and character requirements.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the system rejects invalid email formats and accepts valid ones based on standard email format (local-part@domain) until exact rule is clarified.
Verify that passwords meet length (8-12 characters) and composition (at least one number, one special character, one alphabet character) requirements.

sub_functions.description: Ensures that the password and confirm password fields match during registration.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that an error is raised when password and confirm password do not match, and that submission proceeds when they match.

sub_functions.description: Checks that all validation errors are displayed inline and provide meaningful feedback to the user.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that all validation errors (e.g., invalid email, weak password, mismatched passwords, invalid phone/username, duplicate email) are displayed inline near the respective fields and are descriptive.

sub_functions.description: Validates the format of phone number and username fields. Since exact formats are undefined, tests rely on assumed reasonable patterns and inline error messages.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that invalid phone numbers (e.g., non-numeric, too short, too long) are rejected with an inline error message, and valid ones are accepted based on assumed pattern.
Verify that invalid usernames (e.g., containing special characters, too short, too long) are rejected with an inline error message, and valid alphanumeric ones are accepted based on assumed pattern.

sub_functions.description: Ensures that an account cannot be created with an email that is already registered.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that attempting to register with an already existing email returns an appropriate error (e.g., 409 conflict) and an inline error message is displayed near the email field.

main_functions.description: Manages the email verification process required before first login and activates the account upon successful verification. Covers pre-login blocking, account activation, and verification link processing (expiration, resend, token validity).

sub_functions.description: Enforces that email verification is completed before allowing the first login attempt.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a user with an unverified email cannot log in, and an appropriate error message is shown.

sub_functions.description: Confirms that the account status becomes active only after successful email verification.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that after completing email verification, the account is marked as active and the user can log in.

sub_functions.description: Handles verification link validity, expiration, and resend functionality. Assumptions include a reasonable expiration time (e.g., 24 hours) and ability to resend.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that an expired verification link does not activate the account and displays an appropriate error message.
Verify that a user can request a new verification email and the new link is valid for activation.
Verify that using a malformed or non-existent token fails with an appropriate error message.
